title: [FIRRTL][Inliner] Remove flattenPoint, use inlinedSymbols
body:
The MutableNLA class tracked path modifications using two separate
mechanisms: a BitVector (inlinedSymbols) for inlining and a separate
integer (flattenPoint) for flattening. This dual representation required
special-case logic throughout the code to handle flattening differently
from inlining.

Since flattening is semantically equivalent to inlining all modules from
a given point onwards in the path, we can represent both operations
uniformly using only the inlinedSymbols BitVector. This eliminates the
need for flattenPoint checks and simplifies the path reconstruction logic.

AI-assisted-by: Augment (Claude Sonnet 4.5)
